suspects.name: Charles Harper
suspects.deception_policy: Charles avoids eye contact and fidgets when questioned about the evening.
suspects.voice: I was in the kitchen preparing snacks, sir. The kitchen is a sanctuary of order.

suspects.name: Anna Smith
suspects.deception_policy: Anna speaks openly but deflects any questions about the murder weapon.
suspects.voice: I was dusting books in the library, sir. I do love old books; they're like time machines.

suspects.name: John Doe
suspects.deception_policy: John is vague about his whereabouts but insists on the truth.
suspects.voice: I was reading a book in the library, sir. I can't remember what it was about, though.

suspects.name: Susan Ray
suspects.deception_policy: Susan is overly friendly and offers too many details about the kitchen.
suspects.voice: I was in the kitchen all evening, sir. I can't believe anyone would think me capable of such a deed.

suspects.name: Thomas Brown
suspects.deception_policy: Thomas changes the subject to gardening when questioned about his movements.
suspects.voice: I was out in the garden all evening, sir. The roses are my pride and joy.

locations.name: Blackwood Mansion Library
locations.description: A room filled with ancient books and a grand fireplace.

locations.name: Blackwood Mansion Kitchen
locations.description: The heart of the household, filled with cooking smells and utensils.

locations.name: Blackwood Mansion Garden
locations.description: A lush garden with a variety of flowers and trees.

clues.description: A small bloodstain on the carpet near the fireplace.
Footprints leading from the garden to the library door.
A broken wine glass on the floor near a cupboard.
A note with threatening words written in red ink, found under a book.
An empty vial with a poison label.
A window that was previously open but now closed.

canonical_timeline.description: Charles sneaks into the library and attacks Lady Eleanor with a concealed weapon.
